text: For the purposes of this Regulation, 'personal data' means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'processing' means any operation or set of operations which is performed on personal data or on sets of personal data, whether or not by automated means.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'restriction of processing' means the marking of stored personal data with the aim of limiting their processing in the future.

For the purposes of this Regulation, 'profiling' means any form of automated processing of personal data consisting of the use of personal data to evaluate certain personal aspects relating to a natural person.

For the purposes of this Regulation, 'pseudonymisation' means the processing of personal data in such a manner that the personal data can no longer be attributed to a specific data subject without the use of additional information.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'filing system' means any structured set of personal data which are accessible according to specific criteria.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'controller' means the natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'processor' means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which processes personal data on behalf of the controller.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'recipient' means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or another body, to which the personal data are disclosed.

For the purposes of this Regulation, 'third party' means a natural or legal person, public authority, agency or body other than the data subject, controller, processor and persons who are authorised to process personal data.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'consent' of the data subject means any freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous indication of the data subject's wishes.

For the purposes of this Regulation, 'personal data breach' means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, personal data.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'genetic data' means personal data relating to the inherited or acquired genetic characteristics of a natural person.

For the purposes of this Regulation, 'biometric data' means personal data resulting from specific technical processing relating to the physical, physiological or behavioural characteristics of a natural person.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'data concerning health' means personal data related to the physical or mental health of a natural person.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'main establishment' means the place of central administration in the Union of a controller or processor.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'representative' means a natural or legal person established in the Union who is designated in writing by the controller or processor.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'enterprise' means a natural or legal person engaged in an economic activity.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'group of undertakings' means a controlling undertaking and its controlled undertakings.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'binding corporate rules' means personal data protection policies which are adhered to by a controller or processor.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'supervisory authority' means an independent public authority which is established by a Member State.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'international organisation' means an organisation and its subordinate bodies governed by public international law.
This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data wholly or partly by automated means.
This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data other than by automated means which form part of a filing system.
This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data in the context of the activities of an establishment of a controller or a processor in the Union.
This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data of data subjects who are in the Union by a controller or processor not established in the Union.
This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data by a controller not established in the Union but in a place where Member State law applies by virtue of public international law.
This Regulation does not apply to the processing of personal data in the course of an activity which falls outside the scope of Union law.
This Regulation does not apply to the processing of personal data by the Member States when carrying out activities which fall within the scope of Chapter 2 of Title V of the TEU.
This Regulation does not apply to the processing of personal data by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity.
This Regulation does not apply to the processing of personal data by competent authorities for the purposes of the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences.
Title II of this Regulation lays down the principles relating to the processing of personal data.
The provisions of this Chapter shall apply to all public authorities and bodies at all levels of government.
This Title shall apply to the transfer of personal data to third countries or international organisations.
The provisions of this Regulation are without prejudice to the application of Directive 2000/31/EC, in particular the liability rules of intermediary service providers.
The reference to 'Member State law' in this Regulation means the law of the Member State applicable to the specific processing operation.
For the purposes of the exemption referred to in Article 6, the national competent authority is the authority designated by each Member State.
The term 'public authority' as used in this Regulation includes national, regional and local authorities.
The notion of 'economic activity' referred to in this Article is to be interpreted in accordance with the case-law of the Court of Justice.
The concept of 'establishment' implies the effective and real exercise of activity through stable arrangements.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'waste' means any substance or object which the holder discards or intends or is required to discard.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'hazardous waste' means waste which displays one or more of the hazardous properties listed in Annex III.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'producer' means any person whose activity produces waste.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'holder' means the producer of the waste or the natural or legal person who is in possession of it.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'collection' means the gathering of waste, including the preliminary sorting and preliminary storage.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'recovery' means any operation the principal result of which is waste serving a useful purpose.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'disposal' means any operation which is not recovery even where the operation has a secondary consequence.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'treatment' means recovery or disposal operations, including preparation prior to recovery or disposal.
This Directive applies to all types of waste, with the exception of certain specific categories listed in Article 2.
This Directive does not apply to gaseous effluents emitted into the atmosphere.
This Directive does not apply to radioactive waste, except where explicitly provided.
For the purposes of this Chapter, 'competent authority' means the authority designated by each Member State to carry out the obligations arising from this Regulation.
For the purposes of the application of the derogation in Article 7, the term 'substantial interest' shall be interpreted restrictively.
The definition of 'financial instrument' in this Section includes transferable securities, money-market instruments, and units in collective investment undertakings.
For the purposes of this Title, 'third country' means a country that is not a member of the Union.
The references to 'working day' in this Regulation mean any day other than Saturday, Sunday or a public holiday in the place where the action is to be performed.
The term 'agreement in writing' includes any form of communication that provides a record of the agreement.
For the purposes of this Article, 'effective access' means the right to obtain information without undue delay or cost.
The notion of 'public interest' in this context shall be determined on a case-by-case basis by the competent court.
The term 'subsidiary' for the purposes of this Regulation means a controlled undertaking within the meaning of the applicable company law.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'consumer' means any natural person who is acting for purposes which are outside his trade, business, craft or profession.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'trader' means any natural person or legal entity acting for purposes relating to his trade, business, craft or profession.
This Directive applies to unfair commercial practices occurring before, during and after a commercial transaction in relation to a product.
This Directive does not apply to the contractual rights and obligations of the parties.
The reference to 'product' in this Title includes any goods or service, including immovable property.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'aid scheme' means any act which enables aid to be granted to undertakings.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'undertaking in difficulty' means an undertaking that is unable to recover its viability through its own resources.
The definition of 'state aid' in Article 107 of the Treaty applies for the purposes of this Regulation.
For the purposes of this Decision, 'special categories of personal data' are those referred to in Article 9 of the GDPR.
The term 'data protection officer' means the person designated to perform the tasks referred to in Article 39 of the GDPR.
For the purposes of this Chapter, 'parent undertaking' means an undertaking which has a participating interest in a subsidiary.
The reference to 'the Committee' in the following articles means the committee established by Article 93 of this Regulation.
For the purposes of this Part, 'electronic communications network' means transmission systems and, where applicable, switching or routing equipment.
For the purposes of this Part, 'electronic communications service' means a service normally provided for remuneration which consists wholly or mainly in the conveyance of signals.
The term 'end-user' for the purposes of this Directive means a user not providing public communications networks or services.
For the purposes of this Article, 'universal service' means the minimum set of services of specified quality to which all end-users have access.
The reference to 'Member State' in this context includes its designated regulatory authority.
For the purposes of the calculation in paragraph 2, 'turnover' means the amount derived from the sale of products and the provision of services.
The term 'merger' for the purposes of this Regulation means a concentration within the meaning of Article 3 of the Merger Regulation.
For the purposes of this Title, 'foreign direct investment' means an investment of any kind by a foreign investor.
This Regulation applies to foreign direct investments that are likely to affect security or public order.
This Regulation does not apply to portfolio investments.
The reference to 'national security' in this Article is the sole responsibility of each Member State.
For the purposes of this Protocol, 'substance' means a chemical element and its compounds.
For the purposes of this Protocol, 'preparation' means a mixture or solution composed of two or more substances.
The term 'manufacturer' means any natural or legal person who manufactures a product or has a product designed or manufactured.
The term 'authorised representative' means any natural or legal person established within the Union who has a written mandate from a manufacturer.
The term 'importer' means any natural or legal person established within the Union who places a product from a third country on the Union market.
The term 'distributor' means any natural or legal person in the supply chain, other than the manufacturer or importer, who makes a product available on the market.
The reference to 'harmonised standards' in this Chapter means standards adopted by one of the European standardisation organisations.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'making available on the market' means any supply of a product for distribution, consumption or use on the Union market.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'putting into service' means the first use of a product in the Union by the end-user.
The reference to 'the competent authority' in the implementing provisions is the authority designated by the Member State in accordance with Article 27.
For the purposes of this Section, 'security of network and information systems' means the ability of those systems to resist accidental events or unlawful actions.
The term 'operator of essential services' for the purposes of this Directive means a public or private entity of a type listed in Annex II.
The term 'digital service provider' means any legal person that provides a digital service within the meaning of Article 1(2).
For the purposes of this Chapter, 'incident' means any event having an actual adverse effect on the security of network and information systems.
The reference to 'the Board' means the Cooperation Group established under Article 11.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'the Union institutions' means the institutions, bodies, offices and agencies of the Union.
The term 'official document' for the purposes of this Regulation means any content whatever its medium drawn up or received by an institution.
The reference to 'the Ombudsman' means the European Ombudsman appointed by the European Parliament.
For the purposes of this Title, 'the Court' means the Court of Justice of the European Union, including the General Court.
The term 'appeal' in this Article refers to the remedy provided for in Article 56 of the Statute of the Court of Justice.
For the purposes of this Directive, 'quality standard' means a standard that defines the requirements for quality management systems.
The reference to 'the applicable collective agreement' in this Article shall be determined according to the national law of the host Member State.
For the purposes of this Chapter, 'platform' means a natural or legal person providing a multilateral commercial service.
The term 'recipient of the service' means any natural or legal person who uses the platform for the purpose of offering goods or services.
The reference to 'the register' in this Section means the electronic register maintained by the Commission.
For the purposes of this Decision, 'the designated authority' is the body notified by the Member State to the Commission.
The term 'working time' for the purposes of this Directive means any period during which the worker is working, at the employer's disposal and carrying out his activity.
The reference to 'the social partners' means the organisations representing employers and workers at Union level.
For the purposes of this Protocol, 'adverse effects' means changes in the environment which have harmful effects on human health or on biological diversity.
This Regulation applies to providers placing on the market or putting into service AI systems in the Union.
This Regulation applies to deployers of AI systems that have their place of establishment within the Union.
This Regulation applies to providers and deployers of AI systems that have their place of establishment in a third country, where the output produced by the system is used in the Union.
This Regulation does not apply to AI systems developed or used exclusively for military purposes.
This Regulation does not apply to AI systems used solely for research and innovation purposes.
This Regulation applies to the placing on the market, the putting into service, and the use of high-risk AI systems.
This Directive applies to contracts concluded between a seller and a consumer.
This Directive does not apply to contracts for the supply of water, gas or electricity where they are not put up for sale in a limited volume.
This Directive does not apply to contracts for the supply of digital content which is incorporated in goods.
This Title applies to all undertakings operating in the internal market.
This Chapter applies only to processors established in the Union.
This Section applies to the application of Articles 15 to 20 under specific circumstances.
The obligations laid down in this Article apply only to processing operations that are likely to result in a high risk.
This Article applies only in so far as the processing is carried out by a court or judicial authority.
The provisions relating to the processing of personal data for archiving purposes in the public interest shall apply subject to the conditions set out in this Chapter.
This Regulation shall not apply to the processing of personal data which is not carried out wholly or partly by automated means and which does not form part of a filing system.
The scope of this Directive is limited to distance contracts concluded between the trader and the consumer.
The provisions of this Title shall not affect the application of the rules on competition.
The Commission shall be empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 92 for the purpose of further specifying the criteria and requirements established in this Regulation.

The Commission shall adopt implementing acts laying down the technical specifications for the format and procedure of the information to be provided, in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 93(2).
The provisions of Article 13 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the processing of personal data not obtained from the data subject.
Member State law may determine the conditions under which the provisions of this Regulation may be restricted.
The exemptions provided for in Article 89(1) shall apply to the processing of personal data for archiving purposes in the public interest.
The right to object pursuant to Article 21 shall be brought to the attention of the data subject.
The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 97 to amend the list of high-risk AI systems.
The law of the Member State to which the controller is subject may impose further conditions for the processing of special categories of data.
The provisions relating to the transfer of personal data to third countries shall apply mutatis mutandis to the transfer of data to international organisations.
The power to adopt the delegated acts referred to in Article 10(2) shall be conferred on the Commission for an indeterminate period of time.
The applicable legal basis for the processing shall be determined by Union law or the law of the Member State to which the controller is subject.
The procedure laid down in Article 7 of Directive 89/665/EEC shall apply to the review of decisions taken by the contracting authority.
The obligations set out in Article 32 relating to the security of processing shall be implemented through appropriate technical and organisational measures.
The Commission may adopt delegated acts, in accordance with Article 290 of the TFEU, to supplement this Regulation where necessary.
Member States shall determine the conditions under which processing of personal data for journalistic purposes may be subject to derogations.
The rules on data protection impact assessments laid down in Article 35 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the processor.
The categories of data referred to in Annex I shall be considered as sensitive data for the purposes of this Section.
The delegation of power referred to in Article 42(3) may be revoked at any time by the European Parliament or by the Council.
The competent authority designated by each Member State shall perform the tasks assigned to it under this Regulation in accordance with national law.
The principles set out in Article 5(1) shall apply to all processing operations covered by this Chapter.
The provisions of Article 28 concerning the processor shall apply mutatis mutandis to any sub-processor engaged by the processor.
The details of the registration shall be laid down in implementing acts adopted by the Commission pursuant to Article 54.
The national law of the host Member State may lay down rules relating to the right of Union citizens to vote.
A delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 70 shall enter into force only if no objection has been expressed by the European Parliament.
The notification referred to in Article 33(1) shall be made without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it.
The rights of the data subject referred to in Articles 15 to 18 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the processing for direct marketing purposes.
The determination of whether a third country ensures an adequate level of protection shall be made by the Commission by way of implementing act.
The Commission shall be empowered to adopt delegated acts concerning the calculation of the fees referred to in Article 55.
The provisions of this Directive shall apply without prejudice to the specific rules laid down in Regulation (EC) No 261/2004.
For the purposes of this Article, 'biometric data' means personal data resulting from specific technical processing relating to the physical or physiological characteristics of a natural person.
For the purposes of this Regulation, 'processing' means any operation or set of operations which is performed on personal data or on sets of personal data.
For the purposes of this Chapter, 'establishment' implies the effective and real exercise of activity through stable arrangements.
For the purposes of this Section, 'genetic data' means personal data relating to the inherited or acquired genetic characteristics of a natural person.
The term 'undertaking' for the purposes of this Regulation shall have the same meaning as in Article 101 of the Treaty.
For the purposes of this Protocol, 'adverse effects' means changes in the environment which have harmful effects on human health.
In this Regulation, 'supervisory authority' means an independent public authority which is established by a Member State.
The concept of 'controller' for the purposes of this Section shall be interpreted in accordance with the case-law of the Court of Justice.
The term 'waste' for the purposes of this Chapter means any substance or object which the holder discards or intends to discard.
The term 'manufacturer' for the purposes of this Part means any natural or legal person who manufactures a product or has a product designed or manufactured.
For the purposes of the derogation in Article 7, the term 'substantial interest' shall be interpreted restrictively.
The definitions set out in Article 4 of the GDPR shall apply for the purposes of this Decision.
The term 'importer' in this Section means any natural or legal person established within the Union who places a product from a third country on the Union market.
For the purposes of this Chapter, 'the Board' means the Cooperation Group established under Article 11.
The concept of 'group of undertakings' for the purposes of this Regulation refers to a controlling undertaking and its controlled undertakings.
For the purposes of this Annex, 'economic activity' is to be interpreted in accordance with the case-law of the Court.
The term 'end-user' means a user not providing public communications networks or services.
For the purposes of this Article, 'working day' means any day other than Saturday, Sunday or a public holiday in the place where the action is to be performed.
The notion of 'platform' for the purposes of this Chapter means a natural or legal person providing a multilateral commercial service.
The term 'incident' for the purposes of this Chapter means any event having an actual adverse effect on the security of network and information systems.
The term 'parent undertaking' means an undertaking which has a participating interest in a subsidiary.
The provisions of Chapter III shall apply without prejudice to the specific rules contained in Chapter IV.
The rules of procedure of the Board of Appeal shall be adopted in accordance with the procedure set out in Article 58.
The application of this Article is subject to the general conditions laid down in the framework decision.
The format and manner of the notification shall be determined by the implementing rules referred to in Article 12.
The level of fines shall be calculated in accordance with the methodology established in the delegated acts.
The specific obligations of the data protection officer are further specified in the guidelines adopted by the European Data Protection Board.
The transfer of data under this Section shall be carried out in compliance with the standard contractual clauses approved by the Commission.
The decisions of the supervisory authority may be challenged before the national courts under the conditions set out in national law.
The technical standards for interoperability shall be published in the Official Journal of the European Union.
The procedure for the adoption of the opinion of the Committee shall be governed by Regulation (EU) No 182/2011.
The limitations on the exercise of the rights of the data subject shall be consistent with the essential content of the fundamental rights and freedoms.
The conditions for the renewal of the authorisation shall be laid down in the national implementing legislation.
The arrangements for the cooperation between supervisory authorities shall be established in the cooperation procedure referred to in Article 60.
The content of the data protection impact assessment shall be based on the list published by the supervisory authority.
The criteria for the accreditation of certification bodies shall be defined in the delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 43(3).
The rules on the protection of personal data in the Member States are subject to the general principles of Union law.
The specific requirements for the processing of data concerning health are further elaborated in the codes of conduct referred to in Article 40.
The jurisdiction of the Court of Justice of the European Union shall be exercised in accordance with the provisions of the Treaties.
The final adoption of the implementing measures shall take place after the expiry of the scrutiny period provided for in the applicable regulation.
The liability of the controller shall be determined in accordance with the provisions of Article 82.
The procedure for the settlement of disputes between Member States shall be as provided for in Article 259 of the TFEU.
The designation of the competent authorities shall be notified to the Commission without delay and shall be published in the Official Journal.
The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk.
Each Member State shall designate one or more independent public authorities to supervise the application of this Regulation.
The processor shall process the personal data only on documented instructions from the controller.
The data subject must be provided with information regarding the purposes of the processing at the time the data are collected.
All employers are required to keep records of working time for each employee for a period of at least three years.
The manufacturer shall carry out a conformity assessment procedure before placing the product on the market.
The importer shall indicate on the product its name, registered trade name or registered trademark and the address at which it can be contacted.
The distributor shall act with due care in relation to the requirements of this Directive.
The competent authority shall notify the applicant of its decision within ninety days of receipt of the complete application.
The operator shall ensure that the installation does not cause any significant pollution of the surrounding environment.
The producer shall take back the waste from the final holder free of charge.
The holder of the authorisation shall comply with all conditions attached to that authorisation.
The court shall give reasons for its judgment and shall state the evidence on which it relies.
The public authority shall respond to requests for access to documents within fifteen working days.
The consumer must pay the agreed price in the currency stipulated in the contract.
The seller is required to deliver goods that are in conformity with the contract of sale.
The trader has the duty to inform the consumer of the total price including all taxes and charges before the contract is concluded.
The online platform shall take down illegal content upon receipt of a valid notice.
The carrier shall compensate the sender for any loss or damage to the goods during transit.
The tenant shall maintain the leased premises in good repair throughout the duration of the lease.
The applicant shall submit all supporting documents together with the application form.
The recipient of the grant shall submit a final report on the use of the funds within six months of the project's completion.
The data exporter shall provide the data importer with any information necessary to comply with the contractual obligations.
The beneficiary shall inform the competent authority of any change in the circumstances that might affect the eligibility for the benefit.
No person shall process personal data concerning a data subject without the explicit consent of that data subject.
The controller shall not transfer personal data to a third country unless the Commission has issued an adequacy decision.
The data subject shall not be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her.
Member States shall not impose any prior authorisation or equivalent requirement for the provision of information society services.
The trader shall not charge the consumer any fees for the use of a means of payment that exceed the cost borne by the trader.
No consumer shall be required to waive any of the rights conferred by this Directive.
The seller may not alter the price after the conclusion of the contract unless the parties have expressly agreed on a price revision clause.
The manufacturer is prohibited from placing on the market a product that does not comply with the essential requirements of this Directive.
The supplier shall not supply any substance listed in Annex I to any person unless that person holds a valid authorisation.
The supervisory authority shall not disclose the identity of the complainant to the controller without the complainant's consent.
The court shall not admit evidence obtained in violation of the rights of the defence.
The public official shall not use confidential information obtained in the course of his or her duties for personal gain.
The employer must not dismiss an employee for exercising the right to request flexible working arrangements.
The processor is prohibited from engaging another processor without the prior written authorisation of the controller.
No undertaking shall abuse a dominant position within the internal market.
The competent body shall not refuse to recognise a professional qualification obtained in another Member State on grounds of nationality.
The consumer may withdraw from the contract within fourteen days without giving any reason.

The controller may process personal data for a purpose other than that for which they were collected only if the data subject has given consent or if the processing is based on Union or Member State law.
The supervisory authority may impose an administrative fine in addition to or instead of other corrective measures.
The data subject may request from the controller access to and rectification or erasure of personal data.
The Member States may maintain or introduce further conditions for the processing of genetic data, provided that such conditions are compatible with this Regulation.
The Commission may adopt implementing acts specifying the format and procedures for the exchange of information.
The consumer is permitted to cancel the contract by sending a clear statement to the trader by post, fax or email.
The producer may choose between repair and replacement of a defective product, provided that the chosen remedy is not disproportionate.
The importer is allowed to affix the CE marking to the product after the conformity assessment has been successfully completed.
The competent authority may request any additional information it deems necessary to evaluate the application.
The data subject is free to object at any time to the processing of personal data for direct marketing purposes.
The trader may offer a more extensive guarantee than the minimum required by law.
The Member States are allowed to provide for higher penalties for infringements of this Regulation than those set out in Article 83.
The judge may order the disclosure of the documents in the possession of the opposing party if such disclosure is relevant to the case.
The consumer has the option to seek an out-of-court settlement of the dispute through an ombudsman.
The manufacturer is permitted to designate an authorised representative to perform certain tasks on its behalf.
The supervisory authority is at liberty to cooperate with other supervisory authorities to ensure consistent application of this Regulation.
This Regulation shall not apply to the processing of personal data by a natural person in the course of a purely personal or household activity.
The obligation to provide information to the data subject shall not apply where the data subject already has the information.
The right to erasure under Article 17 shall not apply if the processing is necessary for compliance with a legal obligation.
The provisions on data protection impact assessments shall not apply to processing operations that are not likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.
The requirement to designate a data protection officer shall be waived for courts acting in their judicial capacity.
The prohibition on transferring personal data to third countries shall not prevent the transfer of data necessary for the performance of a contract between the data subject and the controller.
The trader is exempt from the obligation to provide the withdrawal form where the contract is concluded in a physical shop.
The product shall not be subject to the safety requirements of this Directive if it was manufactured before the date of entry into force.
The restriction of the rights of the data subject shall not apply in cases where the processing is carried out for journalistic purposes or for academic expression.
The duty to notify the supervisory authority of a personal data breach shall not apply if the breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.
By way of derogation from the general rule, the consumer shall have a right of withdrawal of twelve months in cases where the trader failed to provide the required information.

The requirement to obtain explicit consent shall not apply to the processing of sensitive data where the data subject is physically or legally incapable of giving consent and the processing is necessary to protect the vital interests of the data subject.
The limitation on the use of automated decision-making shall not apply if the decision is authorised by Union or Member State law.
The provisions of this Directive do not apply to the carriage of passengers by air where the flight is operated on a non-scheduled basis.
The obligation to accept returns shall not extend to products that have been unsealed by the consumer after delivery.
The seller is not required to provide a guarantee for second-hand goods beyond one year from the date of delivery.
The rules on jurisdiction shall not apply to matters relating to the status or legal capacity of natural persons.
The prohibition on discrimination shall not prevent Member States from maintaining differences in treatment based on nationality in the exercise of public authority.
The duty to keep records of processing activities is waived for organisations with fewer than 250 employees.
The requirement for a prior authorisation shall be waived for small-scale clinical trials that involve minimal risk.
The exceptions provided for in this Article shall not apply where the processing is carried out by a public authority.
The general obligation to inform the data subject shall be suspended for as long as the information would compromise an official investigation.
The manufacturer shall be relieved of liability if it can prove that the defect was caused by the product's compliance with mandatory regulatory standards.
The threshold for notification shall be increased for low-risk processing operations, as specified in the guidelines.
The right of access shall be exercised in a manner that does not adversely affect the rights and freedoms of others.
All natural persons shall have the right to vote and to stand as candidates in elections to the European Parliament.
Every citizen has the right to move and reside freely within the territory of the Member States.
The worker has the right to request the employer to provide a written statement of the essential terms of the employment relationship.
The national of a Member State shall enjoy the same social security benefits as the nationals of the host State.
The beneficiary of international protection shall have the right to access employment subject to rules generally applicable to the profession.
The accused has the right to be presumed innocent until proved guilty according to law.
Every person charged with a criminal offence has the right to be informed promptly, in a language which he or she understands, of the nature and cause of the accusation.
The defendant has the right to have adequate time and facilities for the preparation of his or her defence.
The victim of a crime has the right to receive compensation from the offender or the State.
The applicant for asylum has the right to remain in the territory pending the determination of the application.
The child has the right to be heard in any judicial and administrative proceedings affecting the child.
The patient has the right to give informed consent before any medical intervention is performed.
The consumer has the right to receive a copy of the contract concluded with the trader.
The buyer has the right to reject the goods if they are not in conformity with the contract.
The policyholder has the right to cancel the insurance contract within the first fifteen days of the conclusion of the contract.
The shareholder has the right to inspect the books and records of the company.
The employee has the right to be represented by a trade union in collective bargaining.
The author has the right to claim authorship of the work and to object to any distortion of the work.
The patent holder has the right to prevent third parties from making, using or selling the patented invention without consent.
The trademark owner has the right to prevent the registration of a similar sign for identical goods or services.
The data subject has the right to request the controller to transmit the personal data directly to another controller.
The witness has the right to be accompanied by a legal counsel during the testimony.
The taxpayer has the right to appeal against the decision of the tax authority to an independent tribunal.
The consumer has the right to choose between repair, replacement, price reduction or rescission in the case of non-conforming goods.
The passenger has the right to reimbursement of the full cost of the ticket in the event of cancellation of the flight.
The parent has the right to take parental leave of at least four months.
The tenant has the right to sublet the premises with the consent of the landlord, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
The account holder has the right to close the account at any time without penalty.
The patient has the right to access his or her medical records.
The owner of a copyright has the right to authorise or prohibit the reproduction of the work in any form.
The candidate in an election has the right to equal treatment and non-discrimination in the electoral process.
The prisoner has the right to communicate with his or her legal adviser in confidence.
The student has the right to education free of discrimination on any ground.
The worker has the right to a safe and healthy working environment.
The consumer has the right to be protected against unfair commercial practices.
The citizen has the right to petition the European Parliament on any matter that falls within the competence of the Union.
The party to a civil proceeding has the right to be heard and to present evidence in support of his or her case.
The victim of domestic violence has the right to access emergency accommodation and support services.
The person with disabilities has the right to reasonable accommodation in the workplace.
The elderly person has the right to social and medical assistance necessary to maintain a dignified life.

The free movement of personal data within the Union shall be neither restricted nor prohibited for reasons connected with the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data.
Regulation (EC) No 45/2001 and other Union legal acts applicable to such processing of personal data shall be adapted to the principles and rules of this Regulation in accordance with Article 98.
Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject.
Personal data shall be collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes.
Personal data shall be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed.

Personal data shall be accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that personal data that are inaccurate, having regard to the purposes for which they are processed, are erased or rectified without delay.
Personal data shall be kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed.

Personal data shall be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures.
The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, paragraph 1.
Where processing is based on consent, the controller shall be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented to processing of his or her personal data.

If the data subject's consent is given in the context of a written declaration which also concerns other matters, the request for consent shall be presented in a manner which is clearly distinguishable from the other matters, in an intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language.

The controller shall make reasonable efforts to verify in such cases that consent is given or authorised by the holder of parental responsibility over the child, taking into consideration available technology.
Any comprehensive register of criminal convictions shall be kept only under the control of official authority.

The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information referred to in Articles 13 and 14 and any communication under Articles 15 to 22 and 34 relating to processing to the data subject in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language.
The controller shall facilitate the exercise of data subject rights under Articles 15 to 22.
The controller shall provide information on action taken on a request under Articles 15 to 22 to the data subject without undue delay and in any event within one month of receipt of the request.

If the controller does not take action on the request of the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject without delay and at the latest within one month of receipt of the request of the reasons for not taking action and on the possibility of lodging a complaint with a supervisory authority and seeking a judicial remedy.
The controller shall bear the burden of demonstrating the manifestly unfounded or excessive character of the request.

Where personal data relating to a data subject are collected from the data subject, the controller shall, at the time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with the identity and the contact details of the controller and, where applicable, of the controller's representative.

Where the controller intends to further process the personal data for a purpose other than that for which the personal data were collected, the controller shall provide the data subject prior to that further processing with information on that other purpose and with any relevant further information as referred to in paragraph 2.
Where personal data have not been obtained from the data subject, the controller shall provide the data subject with the categories of personal data concerned.

In addition to the information referred to in paragraph 1, the controller shall provide the data subject with the period for which the personal data will be stored, or if that is not possible, the criteria used to determine that period.
The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing.
The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller without undue delay the rectification of inaccurate personal data concerning him or her.

The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay and the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay where one of the grounds applies.

Where the controller has made the personal data public and is obliged to erase the personal data, the controller, taking account of available technology and the cost of implementation, shall take reasonable steps, including technical measures, to inform controllers which are processing the personal data that the data subject has requested the erasure by such controllers of any links to, or copy or replication of, those personal data.
A data subject who has obtained restriction of processing shall be informed by the controller before the restriction of processing is lifted.

The controller shall communicate any rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing carried out in accordance with Article 16, Article 17(1) and Article 18 to each recipient to whom the personal data have been disclosed, unless this proves impossible or involves disproportionate effort.
The controller shall inform the data subject about those recipients if the data subject requests it.

At the latest at the time of the first communication with the data subject, the right referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be explicitly brought to the attention of the data subject and shall be presented clearly and separately from any other information.
Where personal data are processed for direct marketing purposes, the personal data shall no longer be processed for such purposes where the data subject objects.

In the cases referred to in points (a) and (c) of paragraph 2, the data controller shall implement suitable measures to safeguard the data subject's rights and freedoms and legitimate interests, at least the right to obtain human intervention on the part of the controller, to express his or her point of view and to contest the decision.

Taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure and to be able to demonstrate that processing is performed in accordance with this Regulation.
Those measures shall be reviewed and updated where necessary.
Where proportionate in relation to processing activities, the measures referred to in paragraph 1 shall include the implementation of appropriate data protection policies by the controller.

The controller shall, both at the time of the determination of the means for processing and at the time of the processing itself, implement appropriate technical and organisational measures, such as pseudonymisation, which are designed to implement data-protection principles, such as data minimisation, in an effective manner.

The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed.
Joint controllers shall in a transparent manner determine their respective responsibilities for compliance with the obligations under this Regulation by means of an arrangement between them.
The arrangement referred to in paragraph 1 shall duly reflect the respective roles and relationships of the joint controllers vis-à-vis the data subjects.
The essence of the arrangement shall be made available to the data subject.
Where Article 3(2) applies, the controller or the processor shall designate in writing a representative in the Union.

The representative shall be mandated by the controller or processor to be addressed in addition to or instead of the controller or the processor by, in particular, supervisory authorities and data subjects, on all issues related to processing, for the purposes of ensuring compliance with this Regulation.

Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller, the controller shall use only processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that processing will meet the requirements of this Regulation and ensure the protection of the rights of the data subject.
The processor shall not engage another processor without prior specific or general written authorisation of the controller.

The processor shall process the personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, including with regard to transfers of personal data to a third country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law.
The processor shall ensure that persons authorised to process the personal data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality.
The processor shall take all measures required pursuant to Article 32.

The processor shall assist the controller in ensuring compliance with the obligations pursuant to Articles 32 to 36 taking into account the nature of processing and the information available to the processor.

The processor shall, at the choice of the controller, delete or return all the personal data to the controller after the end of the provision of services relating to processing, and delete existing copies unless Union or Member State law requires storage of the personal data.

The processor shall make available to the controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with the obligations laid down in this Article and allow for and contribute to audits, including inspections, conducted by the controller or another auditor mandated by the controller.
The processor shall immediately inform the controller if, in its opinion, an instruction infringes this Regulation or other Union or Member State data protection provisions.

Where that other processor fails to fulfil its data protection obligations, the initial processor shall remain fully liable to the controller for the performance of that other processor's obligations.
The contract or the other legal act referred to in paragraphs 3 and 4 shall be in writing, including in electronic form.

The processor and any person acting under the authority of the controller or of the processor, who has access to personal data, shall not process those data except on instructions from the controller, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law.
Each controller and, where applicable, the controller's representative, shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility.
Each processor and, where applicable, the processor's representative shall maintain a record of all categories of processing activities carried out on behalf of a controller.
The records referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be in writing, including in electronic form.
The controller or the processor and, where applicable, the controller's or the processor's representative, shall make the record available to the supervisory authority on request.
The controller and the processor and, where applicable, their representatives, shall cooperate, on request, with the supervisory authority in the performance of its tasks.

Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risk for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller and the processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk.

The controller and processor shall take steps to ensure that any natural person acting under the authority of the controller or the processor who has access to personal data does not process them except on instructions from the controller, unless he or she is required to do so by Union or Member State law.

In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority competent in accordance with Article 55, unless the personal data breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.
The processor shall notify the controller without undue delay after becoming aware of a personal data breach.
The controller shall document any personal data breaches, comprising the facts relating to the personal data breach, its effects and the remedial action taken.

When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay.

Where a type of processing is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data.
The controller shall seek the advice of the data protection officer, where designated, when carrying out a data protection impact assessment.

The supervisory authority shall establish and make public a list of the kind of processing operations which are subject to the requirement for a data protection impact assessment pursuant to paragraph 1.

Where necessary, the controller shall carry out a review to assess if processing is performed in accordance with the data protection impact assessment at least when there is a change of the risk represented by processing operations.

The controller shall consult the supervisory authority prior to processing where a data protection impact assessment under Article 35 indicates that the processing would result in a high risk in the absence of measures taken by the controller to mitigate the risk.

Member States shall consult the supervisory authority during the preparation of a proposal for a legislative measure to be adopted by a national parliament, or of a regulatory measure based on such a legislative measure, which relates to processing.

The controller and the processor shall designate a data protection officer in any case where the processing is carried out by a public authority or body, except for courts acting in their judicial capacity.
The controller or the processor shall publish the contact details of the data protection officer and communicate them to the supervisory authority.
The controller and the processor shall ensure that the data protection officer is involved, properly and in a timely manner, in all issues which relate to the protection of personal data.

The controller and processor shall support the data protection officer in performing the tasks referred to in Article 39 by providing resources necessary to carry out those tasks and access to personal data and processing operations, and to maintain his or her expert knowledge.
The controller and processor shall ensure that the data protection officer does not receive any instructions regarding the exercise of those tasks.
The data protection officer shall directly report to the highest management level of the controller or the processor.
The data protection officer shall be bound by secrecy or confidentiality concerning the performance of his or her tasks, in accordance with Union or Member State law.

In the absence of a decision pursuant to Article 45(3), a controller or processor may transfer personal data to a third country or an international organisation only if the controller or processor has provided appropriate safeguards, and on condition that enforceable data subject rights and effective legal remedies for data subjects are available.

The controller or processor which submits its processing to the certification mechanism shall provide the certification body with all information and access to its processing activities which are necessary to conduct the certification procedure.
The lead supervisory authority shall, without delay, communicate the relevant information on the matter to the other supervisory authorities concerned.
The lead supervisory authority shall without delay submit a draft decision to the other supervisory authorities concerned for their opinion and take due account of their views.
The lead supervisory authority and the other supervisory authorities concerned shall supply the information required under this Article to each other by electronic means, using a standardised format.
The controller or processor shall take the necessary measures to ensure compliance with the decision as regards processing activities in the context of all its establishments in the Union.
The controller or processor shall notify the measures taken for complying with the decision to the lead supervisory authority, which shall inform the other supervisory authorities concerned.
Each supervisory authority shall, on its territory, monitor and enforce the application of this Regulation.

Each supervisory authority shall handle complaints lodged by a data subject, and investigate, to the extent appropriate, the subject matter of the complaint and inform the complainant of the progress and the outcome of the investigation within a reasonable period.
The performance of the tasks of each supervisory authority shall be free of charge for the data subject and, where applicable, for the data protection officer.

Each member of the supervisory authority shall, during their term of office, refrain from any action incompatible with their duties and shall not, during their term of office, engage in any incompatible occupation, whether gainful or not.
The controller or processor shall compensate any damage which a person may suffer as a result of processing that infringes this Regulation.

The Commission shall publish in the Official Journal of the European Union and on its website a list of the third countries, territories and specified sectors within a third country and international organisations for which it has decided that an adequate level of protection is or is no longer ensured.
The Commission shall designate a representative.
The Chair of the Board shall communicate to the Commission the activities of the Board.
The Board shall ensure the consistent application of this Regulation.

In addition to the obligations listed in Articles 53 and 54, providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall perform model evaluation in accordance with standardised protocols and tools reflecting the state of the art, including conducting and documenting adversarial testing of the model with a view to identifying and mitigating systemic risks.

Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall assess and mitigate possible systemic risks at Union level, including their sources, that may stem from the development, the placing on the market, or the use of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk.

Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall keep track of, document, and report, without undue delay, to the AI Office and, as appropriate, to national competent authorities, relevant information about serious incidents and possible corrective measures to address them.

Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall ensure an adequate level of cybersecurity protection for the general-purpose AI model with systemic risk and the physical infrastructure of the model.

Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risks who do not adhere to an approved code of practice or do not comply with a European harmonised standard shall demonstrate alternative adequate means of compliance for assessment by the Commission.

Providers of data processing services shall take all adequate technical, organisational and legal measures, including contracts, in order to prevent international and third-country governmental access and transfer of non-personal data held in the Union where such transfer or access would create a conflict with Union law or with the national law of the relevant Member State.
The provider of data processing services shall provide the minimum amount of data permissible in response to a request, on the basis of the reasonable interpretation of that request.

The provider of data processing services shall inform the customer about the existence of a request of a third-country authority to access its data before complying with that request, except where the request serves law enforcement purposes and for as long as this is necessary to preserve the effectiveness of the law enforcement activity.

The EDIB referred to in Article 42 shall advise and assist the Commission in developing guidelines on the assessment of whether the conditions laid down in the first subparagraph of this paragraph are met.
The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller without undue delay the rectification of inaccurate personal data concerning him or her.
The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay.
The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller restriction of processing where one of the following applies.
The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, at any time to the processing of personal data concerning him or her.

Where personal data are processed for direct marketing purposes, the data subject shall have the right to object at any time to the processing of personal data concerning him or her for such marketing.
The data subject shall have the right to have incomplete personal data completed, including by means of providing a supplementary statement.

All citizens of the United States shall have the same right, in every State and Territory, as is enjoyed by white citizens thereof to inherit, purchase, lease, sell, hold, and convey real and personal property.
A crime victim has the right to be reasonably protected from the accused.
A crime victim has the right to reasonable, accurate, and timely notice of any public court proceeding, or any parole proceeding, involving the crime.
A crime victim has the right not to be excluded from any such public court proceeding.
A crime victim has the right to be reasonably heard at any public proceeding in the district court involving release, plea, sentencing, or any parole proceeding.
A crime victim has the reasonable right to confer with the attorney for the Government in the case.
A crime victim has the right to full and timely restitution as provided in law.
A crime victim has the right to proceedings free from unreasonable delay.
A crime victim has the right to be treated with fairness and with respect for the victim's dignity and privacy.
A crime victim has the right to be informed in a timely manner of any plea bargain or deferred prosecution agreement.
A crime victim has the right to be informed of the rights under this section.
The court shall ensure that the crime victim is afforded the rights described in subsection (a).
Union citizens who have been deprived of their right to vote or to stand as a candidate should be precluded from exercising that right in the Member State of residence.
Any citizen of the Union, and any natural or legal person residing or having its registered office in a Member State, shall have the right to address a petition to the European Parliament.
Household customers shall be entitled to participate in collective switching schemes.
Member States shall ensure that a customer wishing to switch suppliers is entitled to such a switch within a maximum of three weeks from the date of the request.
Member States shall ensure that the right to switch supplier or market participants engaged in aggregation is granted to customers in a non-discriminatory manner as regards cost, effort and time.
Where a farmer received the holding by way of actual or anticipated inheritance, he shall be entitled to claim the number and the value of payment entitlements.
Any citizen of the Union who holds such a certificate issued in a Member State shall be entitled to gain access.
All citizens of the Union who hold or who are in training to obtain a qualification listed in Annex I(1) shall be entitled to participate in the CTT.

In the case of the death of an individual by the neglect or wrongful act of another in a place subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the United States within a State, a right of action shall exist.
An applicant shall not be deemed to have exhausted the remedies available in the courts of the State if he has the right under the law of the State to raise the question presented.
The Commission shall be entitled to recover the percentage of the annual profit corresponding to the Community contribution.
The seller shall be entitled to terminate the contract and to request that the buyer settles.
The purchaser shall only be entitled with our consent to pass on or sell the delivery items in the ordinary course.
Legal entities of the Union participating in Faroese research programmes shall have the same rights and obligations on intellectual property as those of legal entities established in the Faroes.
The beneficiary shall have the right to request additional pre-financing payments.
Each Party shall have the right to take such measures as may be necessary to protect its essential security interests.
The data importer shall have the right to suspend the transfer of personal data if the law of the third country prevents it from complying with the contract.
The data exporter shall have the right to terminate the contract if the data importer is in breach of its obligations.
The rights holder shall be entitled to claim damages for any infringement of its intellectual property rights.
A person who is the subject of a decision of the Board of Appeal shall have the right to appeal to the Court of Justice.
The accused shall have the right to a speedy and public trial, by an impartial jury of the State and district wherein the crime shall have been committed.
The accused shall be entitled to be confronted with the witnesses against him.
The accused shall have the right to have compulsory process for obtaining witnesses in his favor.
The accused shall have the right to have the assistance of counsel for his defence.
The owner of a copyright shall have the exclusive right to reproduce the copyrighted work in copies.
The owner shall have the exclusive right to prepare derivative works based upon the copyrighted work.
The owner shall have the exclusive right to distribute copies of the copyrighted work to the public by sale or other transfer of ownership.
The owner shall have the exclusive right to perform the copyrighted work publicly.
The owner shall have the exclusive right to display the copyrighted work publicly.
The owner shall have the exclusive right to perform the copyrighted work publicly by means of a digital audio transmission.
Any person who has been subjected to a final judgment in a criminal procedure shall have the right to have that judgment reviewed by a higher tribunal.
Every citizen of the Union shall have the right to move and reside freely within the territory of the Member States.
The national of a Member State shall have the right to vote and to stand as a candidate in elections to the European Parliament in the Member State in which he or she resides.
The national of a Member State shall have the right to vote and to stand as a candidate in municipal elections in the Member State in which he or she resides.
A worker who moves to another Member State shall have the right to remain in that State after having been employed there for a certain period.
The family members of a Union citizen shall have the right to accompany or join the Union citizen in the host Member State.
A Union citizen shall have the right to permanent residence in the host Member State after a continuous period of five years.
The host Member State shall grant the right of permanent residence to the family members of the Union citizen who are not nationals of a Member State.
The worker shall be entitled to social security benefits in the same way as the nationals of the host State.
The self-employed person shall have the right to take up and pursue an activity in another Member State.
The applicant for international protection shall have the right to remain in the Member State pending the examination of the application.
The applicant shall have the right to be informed in a language which he or she understands of the procedure to be followed.
The applicant shall have the right to an effective remedy before a court or tribunal against a decision rejecting the application.
The applicant shall have the right to be provided with a legal counsellor or advisor at any stage of the procedure.
The minor applicant shall have the right to be represented by a legal representative or a person responsible for his or her welfare.
The beneficiary of international protection shall have the right to a residence permit which shall be valid for at least three years.
The beneficiary shall have the right to access employment subject to rules generally applicable to the profession.
The beneficiary shall have the right to education, including training and retraining.
The beneficiary shall have the right to social welfare assistance provided to nationals of the Member State.
The beneficiary shall have the right to medical care, including psychological care, under the same conditions as nationals.
The beneficiary shall have the right to adequate housing under the same conditions as nationals.
The beneficiary shall have the right to integrate into the local community and to participate in civic life.
The Member State shall have the power to adopt implementing rules for this Regulation.
The Commission shall be empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 290 to supplement this Regulation.
The Commission shall be empowered to adopt implementing acts laying down technical specifications.
The Council shall have the power to amend the list of third countries based on the assessment of the Commission.
The competent authority shall have the power to request any information necessary for the performance of its supervisory duties.
The supervisory authority shall have the power to carry out investigations in the form of data protection audits.
The supervisory authority shall have the power to review certifications issued pursuant to Article 42.
The supervisory authority shall have the power to impose a temporary or definitive limitation including a ban on processing.
The supervisory authority shall have the power to order the rectification or erasure of personal data.
The supervisory authority shall have the power to impose an administrative fine pursuant to Article 83.
The supervisory authority shall have the power to bring proceedings before the national courts.
The Member State shall have the power to designate the competent authorities for the application of this Directive.
The national parliament shall have the power to review the draft legislative acts of the Union on the principle of subsidiarity.
The Court of Justice shall have unlimited jurisdiction to review penalties imposed by the Commission.
The Commission shall be empowered to adopt delegated acts concerning the level of fines.
The agency shall have the power to carry out inspections on its own initiative.
The inspector shall have the power to enter the premises of the undertaking during normal working hours.
The inspector shall have the power to seal any premises or records for the period necessary for the inspection.
The investigator shall have the power to take copies of or extracts from any relevant documents.
The Board of Appeal shall have the power to confirm, reverse, or amend the decision under appeal.
The competent authority shall have the power to suspend or prohibit the marketing of a product.
The regulatory body shall have the power to revoke an authorisation if the conditions are no longer fulfilled.
The national court shall have the power to refer a question to the Court of Justice for a preliminary ruling.
The judge shall have the power to grant interim measures to preserve the applicant's interests.
The court shall have the power to order the production of evidence by the opposing party.
The court shall have the power to award costs against the unsuccessful party.
The arbitrator shall have the power to determine the applicable law to the dispute.
The official shall have the power to issue a certificate of conformity.
The designated authority shall have the power to approve the safety reports submitted by the operator.
The supervisory board shall have the power to issue guidelines and recommendations.
The national regulatory authority shall have the power to require the undertaking to provide specific data.
The public prosecutor shall have the power to initiate criminal proceedings for the offences established in this Code.
The investigating judge shall have the power to order the seizure of assets derived from the offence.
The customs authority shall have the power to detain goods suspected of infringing intellectual property rights.
The border control officer shall have the power to refuse entry to a third-country national who does not meet the conditions.
The competition authority shall have the power to accept commitments offered by the undertakings concerned.
The minister shall have the power to authorise exceptions to the general prohibition for reasons of public interest.
The director shall have the power to allocate budgetary resources among the departments.
The committee shall have the power to adopt its own rules of procedure.
The executive director shall have the power to appoint staff in accordance with the Staff Regulations.
The governing council shall have the power to adopt the annual budget of the agency.

Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage as a result of an infringement of this Regulation shall have the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor for the damage suffered.
Any controller involved in processing shall be liable for the damage caused by processing which infringes this Regulation.

A processor shall be liable for the damage caused by processing only where it has not complied with obligations of this Regulation specifically directed to processors or where it has acted outside or contrary to lawful instructions of the controller.
A controller or processor shall be exempt from liability under paragraph 2 if it proves that it is not in any way responsible for the event giving rise to the damage.

Each supervisory authority shall ensure that the imposition of administrative fines pursuant to this Article in respect of infringements of this Regulation referred to in paragraphs 4, 5 and 6 shall in each individual case be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.
Administrative fines shall, depending on the circumstances of each individual case, be imposed in addition to, or instead of, measures referred to in points (a) to (h) and (j) of Article 58(2).

Infringements of the basic principles for processing, including conditions for consent, pursuant to Articles 5, 6, 7 and 9 shall be subject to administrative fines up to 20 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 4 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher.

Infringements of the obligations of the controller and the processor pursuant to Articles 8, 11, 25 to 39 and 42 and 43 shall be subject to administrative fines up to 10 000 000 EUR, or in the case of an undertaking, up to 2 % of the total worldwide annual turnover of the preceding financial year, whichever is higher.

Non-compliance with the prohibition of the AI practices referred to in Article 5 shall be subject to administrative fines of up to 35 000 000 EUR or, if the offender is an undertaking, up to 7 % of its total worldwide annual turnover for the preceding financial year, whichever is higher.

Non-compliance with obligations of providers other than those laid down in Article 5 shall be subject to administrative fines of up to 15 000 000 EUR or, if the offender is an undertaking, up to 3 % of its total worldwide annual turnover for the preceding financial year, whichever is higher.

Non-compliance with the prohibition of the AI practices referred to in Article 5 shall be subject to administrative fines of up to EUR 1 500 000 in the case of Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies.
The non-compliance of the AI system with any requirements or obligations under this Regulation, other than those laid down in Article 5, shall be subject to administrative fines of up to EUR 750 000.
The producer shall be liable for damage caused by a defect in his product.
Where two or more persons are liable for the same damage, they shall be liable jointly.
The producer may be liable even without negligence or fault on their part.
The injured person does not have to prove the negligence or fault of the producer or importer.
Where the injured person is at fault, the producer's liability may be reduced.
No contractual clause may allow the producer to limit his liability in relation to the injured person.
The supplier of a defective product is liable in all cases and on the same basis as the producer.
The legal person shall be civilly liable for payment of the administrative fine imposed on its managers, executives, employees or agents.
